data.title: CAC 40 Lifted by Fincancial and Luxury Stocks
data.description:
The CAC 40 rose 0.8% to close at 8,096 on Wednesday, a one week high as rising bets on a Fed rate cut after weaker US data underpinned risk appetite.

Financials and luxury names led the advance, with AXA, BNP Paribas, Crédit Agricole and Société Générale adding between 0.7% and 2.1%.

Luxury houses also outperformed, LVMH up 1%, Hermès up 1.2%, L’Oréal up 1.8% and Essilor up 1.4%.

Safran gained 1% after it announced a joint venture with Bharat Electronics in India to expand civil and military aerospace operations, while Airbus climbed 1.5%.

On the downside Pernod Ricard fell 3% as the company paid its final €2.35 per share dividend for fiscal 2025.

data.title: Telus' Dividend Rate Seen Safe From Cuts — Market Talk
data.description: Telus' dividend outlook remains sustainable, TD Cowen analyst Vince Valentini says. Management's commitment to monetize noncore assets and reduce debt should lower interest costs and allow free cash flow to fully pay for dividends by 2027. Concerns about a potential dividend cut, similar to BCE's, are misplaced, Valentini says. Telus is working with a lower post-lease payout ratio than BCE pre-dividend cut, as well as higher fiber penetration that reduces capex, and a revenue mix with organic growth potential. What's more, Valentini expects these factors to drive a recovery in Telus' share price, and says even at a 7% dividend yield, which is above BCE's and nearly double Rogers', the stock could trade at around C$24. Shares are up less than 1% on the day at C$18.32. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

data.title: Brazil forecasts record chicken output despite bird flu outbreak
data.description:
By Ana Mano

Brazil's chicken meat production will reach a record-high 15.86 million metric tons in 2026, Brazilian crop agency Conab forecast on Wednesday, a year after its bird flu outbreak.

Conab estimates Brazilian chicken meat output this year at 15.5 million tons, above the 15.4 million tons forecast by poultry and pork industry lobby group ABPA.

Either figure would represent record chicken meat output in the world's largest exporter this year.

The data underscores the resilience of meat companies, including global firms JBS and MBRF , as Brazil grappled with temporary trade embargos from China, Europe and other large buyers after the country reported its first bird flu outbreak on a commercial farm.

"Sales on the international market were affected by a case of avian influenza in May in Rio Grande do Sul," Conab said. "Even so, shipments remained strong, with other markets absorbing a significant chunk of production, mitigating the impact on exports."

Highly pathogenic avian influenza, or bird flu, has defied poultry industry executives and governments worldwide, leading to the death of hundreds of millions of birds in recent years, and disrupting supply and global trade.

Brazil's strong chicken meat production outlook has boosted domestic availability, even with slightly higher exports, Conab said.

The agency estimated shipments this year of 5.2 million tons, higher than the 5.15 million tons in 2024 and in line with the top range of ABPA's August estimate.

ABPA will release fresh projections for Brazilian chicken, pork and egg production and exports on December 3.

China, a top destination for Brazil's chicken meat, lifted the bird-flu-related embargo on Brazilian imports this month.

data.title: Brokers turn more bullish towards gilts, pound after UK budget
data.description:
Some brokers shifted their trade recommendations in favour of British government bonds and the pound on Wednesday, after the country's finance minister Rachel Reeves announced a big tax raising budget .

Nomura said it had closed its recommendation to buy the euro against sterling as the budget "avoided the major tail risks that could have hurt" the pound.

They said the budget's "overall fiscal 'headroom'” was increased, and so anticipate short sterling positions — bets the currency will fall — that had been popular going into the budget will be removed.

That would help the pound rise against the euro. The common currency dropped to as low as 87.55 pence on Wednesday, its lowest in nearly a month.

Meanwhile, Mizuho recommended positioning for the 10-year British government bond, or gilt, to outperform the equivalent 10-year U.S. Treasury.

They said Reeves' budget "knocks off 0.3% from CPI in 2026; allowing for a more dovish BoE outlook."

Dovish is central bank jargon for being more willing to pursue rate cuts, something that supports government bonds.

British 10-year yields dropped 6 basis points on Wednesday, to 4.43%, while the 10-year Treasury yield was flat at 4.01%. (GB10YT=RR),

data.title: Cotton prices hit over 1-week high ahead of Thanksgiving holiday
data.description:
ICE cotton futures climbed to an over one-week high on Wednesday, with trading activity slowing ahead of Thursday's U.S. Thanksgiving holiday.

* Cotton contracts for March (CTH6) rose 0.57 cent, or 0.9%, at 64.8 cents per lb at 11:11 a.m. ET (1611 GMT). The contract hit its highest level since November 18 earlier today.

* "It's a very thin volume because of the holiday tomorrow and into Friday we're going to trade partial day," said Keith Brown, principal at cotton broker Keith Brown and Co in Georgia.

* The market will be closed on Thursday in observance of the Thanksgiving holiday and will operate on a shortened schedule on Friday.

* "Overall, the trend of cotton is down. It's trying to go sideways... range would be from the low of 63 cents to maybe up to 65 cents," Brown added.

* In the grains market, Chicago soybean futures were almost unchanged as traders adjusted positions before the Thanksgiving holiday while remaining cautious about Chinese purchases of U.S. beans under a bilateral trade truce.

* On Friday, the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) is set to release its export sales report for week ended October 16. The data was delayed due to a 43-day U.S. government shutdown, the longest in the country's history, which ended on November 12.

* Earlier this week, research firm BMI said it expects cotton futures prices to hold steady, maintaining its average annual forecasts at 67 cents per pound for 2025 and 70 cents for 2026.

* Looking ahead, the company expects a tighter market balance in 2026, supported by mixed supply prospects amid stable consumption.

data.title: ASUP: Net loss in Q3, but strong loan growth and easing conditions set stage for 2026 recovery
data.description:
Q3 2025 saw a net loss due to margin compression and high costs, but strong loan and deposit growth, especially in corporates, and easing monetary conditions signal recovery. Asset quality weakened but is expected to improve, with 2026 guidance pointing to robust loan growth and higher ROE.

data.title: Backloaded U.K. Fiscal Measures Could Mean Lower Interest Rates Longer Term — Market Talk
data.description: Some of the U.K. budget measures will come into effect in the coming years, indicating interest-rate cuts from the Bank of England that should help push longer-dated gilts lower, Invesco's Arnab Das says in a note. Increased expectations of BOE rate cuts "would validate lower long-term yields", Das says. Thirty-year gilt yields fall to 13-day low of 5.203%, while yields on 10-year gilts fall to a 13-day low of 4.414%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Russia's October car output down 46.7% y/y, Rosstat says
data.description:
Russian production of passenger cars declined by 46.7% year on year in October to 50,000 vehicles, federal statistics service Rosstat said on Wednesday.

Russian carmakers have shortened the work week, struggling to find buyers for their vehicles as the market is expected to decline by about 20% year on year in 2025.

data.title: Corn basis mixed, soy lower in sluggish trade
data.description:
Basis bids for corn were mixed and soy bids were steady to lower in the U.S. Midwest on Wednesday in slow trade ahead of Thursday's Thanksgiving holiday.

  • Movement has been slow and processing margins weaker.

  • Markets will be closed on Thursday.

  • The oilseed market remained underpinned by further indications of Chinese demand, with traders telling Reuters that China bought at least 10 cargoes of U.S. soybeans since Tuesday.

  • That followed a call on Monday between presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping, during which Trump said he had pressed Xi to accelerate and increase Beijing's purchases of U.S. goods.

  • Chicago Board of Trade March corn (CH26) was last up 6-1/2 cents to $4.45 a bushel and January soybeans (SF26) were 6-1/2 cents higher to $11.31-1/4 a bushel.

data.title: Autodesk Seen as Well Positioned for Long-Term Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Autodesk's momentum from a solid third quarter of sales is likely to carry into next year and beyond, Mizuho analyst Siti Panigrahi writes in a note. The digital-design company's architecture, engineering, construction and operations revenue remains strong, and friction is easing from its transition to a new transaction model, Panigrahi writes. In the long term, Autodesk is prioritizing artificial intelligence and cloud work while modernizing its tech stack, and management has remained disciplined about operating expenses, Panigrahi writes. "Given the smooth roll-out of the new transaction model and improving competitive positioning, we remain confident that Autodesk can accelerate its growth and profitability in the long run," he writes. Autodesk shares are up 3.2%. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Trump wins dismissal of Georgia 2020 election interference case
data.description:
A judge on Wednesday agreed to dismiss charges against U.S. President Donald Trump and his allies for allegedly trying to overturn the Republican's 2020 presidential election defeat in Georgia, according to court documents.

Prosecutor Peter Skandalakis had asked for a dismissal of the 39 charges, including racketeering, against Trump and a group of aides, saying prosecuting the case would be "unproductive."

"I recognize that, given the deep political divisions in our country, this decision will not be universally popular," wrote Skandalakis, who earlier this month replaced Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis after she was disqualified last year.

Steve Sadow, a lawyer for Trump, praised the dismissal, saying the case should have never been brought.

The case was one of four criminal prosecutions that Trump faced in the years since losing his 2020 presidential re-election bid to Democrat Joe Biden. Just one — a New York case over a hush money payment to a porn star during his 2016 campaign — went to trial. He was found guilty in the New York case but has asked for the case to be thrown out.

data.title: Magnum Ice Cream raises $3.5 billion in bond issue, Unilever spinoff nears
data.description:
The Magnum Ice Cream Company on Wednesday said it had completed a 3 billion euro ($3.48 billion) debut bond issue that was oversubscribed seven times, less than two weeks before the company will finalise a spinoff from Unilever and list in Amsterdam.

Magnum, which also includes brands such as Wall's and Ben & Jerry's, is ready to tackle challenges from logistics to the rising popularity of weight-loss drugs when it goes solo, its supply chain head told Reuters this week.

Magnum expects to make productivity and efficiency savings as it transitions to a pure-play ice cream business and is betting on consumers continuing to crave indulgent snacks even as healthy eating trends gain prominence.

"The level of interest shown by the market was fantastic, with the order book being oversubscribed by over seven times," CFO Abhijit Bhattacharya said in a statement.

The bond was issued across four tranches of 750 million euros each, Magnum said, due in 2029, 2031, 2034, and 2037, at interest rates ranging from 2.75% to 4%.

Magnum said the issue's net proceeds would be used for "general corporate purposes," including facilitating the demerger from Unilever, which is due to be completed on December 6. Magnum's shares will begin trading on December 8.

($1 = 0.8625 euros)
